translation_text: The people do not fear death;
translation_text_raw: The people do not fear death;

translation_text: to what purpose is it to (try to) frighten them with death?
translation_text_raw: to what purpose is it to (try to) frighten them with death?

chinese_text: 若使民常畏死，而為奇者，吾得執而殺之，孰敢？
translation_text: If the people were always in awe of death, and I could always seize those who do wrong, and put them to death, who would dare to do wrong?
translation_text_raw: If the people were always in awe of death, and I could always seize those who do wrong, and put them to death, who would dare to do wrong?

translation_text: There is always One who presides over the infliction death.
translation_text_raw: There is always One who presides over the infliction death.

chinese_text: 夫代司殺者殺，是謂代大匠斲。
translation_text: He who would inflict death in the room of him who so presides over it may be described as hewing wood instead of a great carpenter.
translation_text_raw: He who would inflict death in the room of him who so presides over it may be described as hewing wood instead of a great carpenter.

chinese_text: 夫代大匠斲者，希有不傷其手矣。
translation_text: Seldom is it that he who undertakes the hewing, instead of the great carpenter, does not cut his own hands!
translation_text_raw: Seldom is it that he who undertakes the hewing, instead of the great carpenter, does not cut his own hands!
